sourceMovie.title: Drive
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Drive works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Usual Suspects
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Drive clicked for you, The Usual Suspects (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Drive?
faq.answer: Try The Usual Suspects, Jackie Brown, Collateral—each shares DNA with Drive while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Drive?
faq.answer: Drive (2011) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Drive worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
